authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Eldorado National Forest (Desolation Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Middle Velma Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Middle Velma Lake not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: CDFW Memorandum — Pre-stocking Evaluation at Middle Velma Lake, El Dorado County, Desolation Wilderness (M. Lockhart, 12/20/2017)
sources.quotedText: Middle Velma Lake is a well-known trophy rainbow trout fishery and has earned a distinguished reputation amongst Desolation Wilderness anglers. It has been stocked with rainbow trout since the 1940s without any variation in species. ... Therefore, rainbow trout will no longer be aerially planted at Middle Velma Lake and CDFW will manage the lake as a Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frog breeding site.

sources.title: Desolation Wilderness Permits — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use. ... HIGH USE SEASON A quota in place from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 of each year.

parameters.note: Middle Velma Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1) (open all year). Although it lies in the Lake Tahoe Basin region, it is NOT a Lake Tahoe tributary: CDFW's field memo states the lake 'drains west into the headwaters of the Rubicon River' (American River watershed), so the §7.50(b)(152) Tahoe-tributary catch-and-release closure does not apply. Only the neighboring Upper and Lower Velma lakes drain east to Emerald Bay / Lake Tahoe.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water and not a Lake Tahoe tributary)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Fishery status: Middle Velma Lake was a well-known put-and-grow, aerially-stocked trophy rainbow trout fishery, planted with rainbow trout from the 1940s through 2007. Following a 2016 pre-stocking survey that found Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frogs (Rana sierrae) breeding and only a single rainbow x golden trout hybrid, CDFW discontinued stocking and now manages the lake as a frog breeding site; the lake is largely devoid of fish. This is a fishery-management status, not a take restriction — general statewide lake take rules still legally apply.

raw.current_management: Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frog breeding site

regulations.citation: CDFW Pre-stocking Evaluation Memo, Middle Velma Lake (12/20/2017)
regulations.humanSummary: Formerly a stocked trophy rainbow trout fishery (planted 1940s–2007); CDFW has discontinued stocking and manages the lake as a Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frog breeding site — it is now largely devoid of fish.
regulations.verbatimText: Therefore, rainbow trout will no longer be aerially planted at Middle Velma Lake and CDFW will manage the lake as a Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frog breeding site.

parameters.description: Middle Velma Lake lies within the Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest). A USDA Forest Service wilderness permit is required for overnight use year-round; during the high-use quota season (Friday before Memorial Day through September 30) overnight visits are subject to a zone quota and day-use permits are required at the trailheads.

raw.quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USFS Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Wilderness Permits
regulations.humanSummary: Access is inside the Desolation Wilderness: a Forest Service wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use, with an overnight zone quota in effect from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30.
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.
